subtopics.title: What's in the Box
subtopics.content:
#### Core Components
- **Town board** — 1 central board showing the village of Flameburgh with 6 starting shops.
- **Dragon meeples** — 1 per player in distinct colors.
- **Artisan dragon cards** — Deck of small dragons that staff the shops, each showing a good type they produce.
- **Fancy dragon cards** — Powerful one-time-use dragons worth reputation points.
- **Enchantment cards** — Cards placed on shops providing permanent bonuses and end-game VP.
- **Goods tokens** — 6 types: bread, iron, meat, potion, plant, crystal.
- **Coin tokens** — Used as wild goods and for certain abilities.
- **Reputation point tokens** — Track scoring throughout the game.
- **Shop cards** — Additional shops added to the town as the game progresses.
- **Charm dragon cards** — End-game scoring objective cards based on collections.
- **First player token** — Dragon-themed start player marker.

#### Player-Count Notes
--- 1 Player ---
- **Solo mode** — Uses an automa deck to simulate an opponent.

--- 2-5 Players ---
- **Standard mode** — All components used; no scaling adjustments to shop count at start.

subtopics.title: Board Layout
subtopics.content:
#### Town Setup
1. **Place town board** — Central play area showing 6 starting shops arranged in a village layout.
2. **Stock shops** — Place artisan dragon cards face-up in each shop's dragon slots as indicated by setup instructions.
3. **Prepare goods supply** — Sort goods tokens by type into accessible piles.
4. **Shuffle enchantment deck** — Place face-down near the board; reveal a market row of available enchantments.
5. **Shuffle fancy dragon deck** — Place face-down near the board; reveal a display of available fancy dragons.
6. **Prepare shop deck** — Shuffle remaining shop cards for later town expansion.
7. **Set charm dragon display** — Reveal charm dragon objective cards for end-game goals.

#### Shop Anatomy
- **Dragon slots** — Each shop has spaces for artisan dragons that determine goods produced.
- **Enchantment slot** — Each shop has one slot for an enchantment card.
- **Shop type icon** — Indicates what category of goods the shop deals in.
- **Shop bonus** — Some shops grant a bonus action when visited.

subtopics.title: Player Setup
subtopics.content:
#### Per-Player Setup
- **Choose color** — Take your dragon meeple in your chosen color.
- **Starting hand** — Draw a starting hand of artisan dragon cards (typically 3).
- **Starting goods** — Receive a set of starting goods tokens as indicated by player order.
- **Charm dragons** — Each player receives a starting charm dragon card as a secret end-game objective.
- **Reputation track** — Set reputation marker to 0.

#### First Player
- **Determine start player** — Give the first player token to the player who most recently saw a dragon (or choose randomly).
- **Turn order** — Play proceeds clockwise from the first player.

subtopics.title: Starting the Game
subtopics.content:
#### Game Start
1. **First player takes first turn** — Place your dragon meeple at any shop in the village.
2. **Opening availability** — All 6 starting shops are available from the first turn.
3. **No blocked shops** — Multiple players may visit the same shop; there is no blocking.

#### Key Starting Rules
- **Dragon meeple placement** — Your meeple starts off the board; on your first turn you place it at a shop.
- **Immediate action** — Upon placing at a shop, you immediately perform your chosen action (Gather or Enchant).

subtopics.title: Turn Structure
subtopics.content:
#### Turn Sequence
1. **Place dragon meeple** — Move your dragon meeple to any shop in the village (may revisit shops).
2. **Fire up dragons** — Optionally place artisan dragon cards from your hand into empty slots at the shop. Gain a reward for each dragon placed (usually 1 good matching the shop type).
3. **Choose action** — Perform exactly one of two actions: **Gather** or **Enchant**.
4. **Draw** — After your action, you may draw artisan dragon cards if your hand is below your hand limit.

#### Action: Gather
- **Collect goods** — Take goods matching each artisan dragon currently at the shop (including any you just placed).
- **Activate shop bonus** — If the shop has a bonus ability, you may use it.
- **Coins as wilds** — Coins can substitute for any good type when spending.

#### Action: Enchant
- **Pay cost** — Spend the goods shown on an available enchantment card.
- **Place enchantment** — Attach the enchantment card to the current shop (shop must have an empty enchantment slot).
- **Earn reputation** — Gain the reputation points printed on the enchantment card.
- **Activate enchantment effect** — Some enchantments grant an immediate bonus upon placement.
- **Town expansion** — After enchanting, a new shop may be added to the village if the shop deck has cards remaining.

subtopics.title: Actions & Options
subtopics.content:
#### Placing Artisan Dragons
- **When** — At the start of your turn, before choosing Gather or Enchant.
- **Requirement** — The shop must have an empty dragon slot.
- **Reward** — Gain 1 good matching the shop type per dragon placed.
- **Hand management** — Placing dragons depletes your hand; balance placement with future needs.

#### Fancy Dragons
- **Acquisition** — Spend goods to claim a fancy dragon from the display.
- **One-time ability** — Each fancy dragon has a powerful effect used once when claimed.
- **Reputation value** — Fancy dragons are worth reputation points at end of game.
- **Replenish** — Replace claimed fancy dragon from the deck.

#### Charm Dragons
- **Secret objectives** — Each charm dragon scores bonus reputation at end of game based on specific collection criteria.
- **Acquisition** — Gain additional charm dragons through certain shop bonuses and abilities.
- **Examples** — Score for number of enchantments placed, for having goods of specific types, for dragons at specific shop types.

#### Coins
- **Wild goods** — Coins substitute for any single good when paying costs.
- **Earning coins** — Some shop bonuses, enchantments, and dragon abilities grant coins.
- **End-game value** — Remaining coins may be worth reputation points.

subtopics.title: Special Mechanics
subtopics.content:
#### Shop Expansion
- **Trigger** — When a shop is enchanted, a new shop card is drawn and added to the village.
- **Growing village** — The village expands over the course of the game, providing more options.
- **New dragon slots** — New shops arrive with empty dragon slots and enchantment slots.

#### No Blocking
- **Open movement** — Any number of players can visit the same shop on consecutive turns.
- **Community building** — Players collectively build up shops by adding dragons and enchantments that benefit everyone who visits.

#### Hand Limit
- **Card management** — Players have a hand limit for artisan dragon cards (typically 6).
- **Drawing** — Draw back toward your limit at the end of your turn if below it.

#### Solo Mode
- **Automa opponent** — A deck of cards drives an automated opponent that claims enchantments and fancy dragons.
- **Scoring comparison** — Compare your final reputation to the automa's total.

#### Reputation Track
- **Running score** — Reputation points are tracked openly throughout the game.
- **Multiple sources** — Enchantments, fancy dragons, charm dragons, and certain bonuses all contribute.

subtopics.title: Endgame & Scoring
subtopics.content:
#### End-Game Trigger
- **Enchantment depletion** — The game ends when the enchantment deck runs out or a set number of enchantments have been placed.
- **Final round** — All players complete an equal number of turns after the trigger.

#### Final Scoring
1. **Reputation earned during play** — Points from enchantments placed and fancy dragons claimed.
2. **Fancy dragon values** — Add reputation printed on each fancy dragon card you own.
3. **Charm dragon bonuses** — Score each charm dragon based on its collection criteria.
4. **Remaining coins** — Convert leftover coins to reputation if applicable.
5. **Total** — Sum all reputation points. Highest total wins.

#### Tiebreaker
- **Goods remaining** — Player with more remaining goods wins ties.
- **Further tie** — Player with more artisan dragons in hand breaks further ties.

subtopics.title: Opening Priorities
subtopics.content:
#### Early Game Focus
- **Stock shops first** — Place artisan dragons early to build up shops for bigger Gather payoffs later.
- **Prioritize charm dragons** — Acquiring charm dragon objectives early lets you plan your entire game around them.
- **Gather before Enchant** — Build a goods reserve in the first few turns before spending on enchantments.
- **Read the shop layout** — Identify which shops synergize with your starting charm dragon objectives.

#### Resource Tempo
- **Coins are premium** — Treat coins as strategic reserves; do not spend them on basic costs when specific goods are available.
- **Dragon placement timing** — Place dragons at shops you plan to Gather from soon, maximizing the double reward (placement bonus + Gather yield).

subtopics.title: Common Mistakes
subtopics.content:
#### Mistake 1 — Enchanting Too Early
- **Why it fails** — Spending scarce early goods on enchantments leaves you with no reserves for fancy dragons or future enchantments.
- **Fix** — Gather efficiently for 2-3 turns before your first enchantment.

#### Mistake 2 — Ignoring Charm Dragons
- **Why it fails** — Charm dragons can be worth significant end-game points; ignoring them leaves points on the table.
- **Fix** — Acquire 2-3 charm dragons and orient your strategy around their scoring conditions.

#### Mistake 3 — Hoarding Artisan Dragons
- **Why it fails** — Dragons in hand produce nothing; dragons on shops produce goods for everyone including you.
- **Fix** — Place dragons frequently, especially at shops you intend to revisit.

#### Mistake 4 — Neglecting Fancy Dragons
- **Why it fails** — Fancy dragons provide both immediate powerful abilities and end-game reputation.
- **Fix** — Budget goods for at least 2-3 fancy dragon purchases per game.

#### Mistake 5 — Spreading Too Thin
- **Why it fails** — Visiting many different shops wastes turns on movement without compounding benefits.
- **Fix** — Focus on 3-4 key shops that align with your charm objectives and enchantment plans.

subtopics.title: Key Decisions
subtopics.content:
#### Decision 1 — Gather vs Enchant Timing
- **Gather line** — Stockpile goods for a big enchantment turn or fancy dragon purchase.
- **Enchant line** — Lock in reputation points and expand the village before opponents claim the best slots.

#### Decision 2 — Charm Dragon Diversity vs Focus
- **Focused approach** — Pursue charm dragons with overlapping criteria for consistent scoring.
- **Diverse approach** — Spread across different criteria to hedge against blocked paths.

#### Decision 3 — Dragon Placement Generosity
- **Place freely** — Building up shops benefits you on future visits and earns placement rewards.
- **Hold strategically** — Keep key dragons in hand if opponents would benefit more from a stocked shop.

#### Decision 4 — Fancy Dragon Timing
- **Early fancy dragon** — Powerful ability used over more remaining turns.
- **Late fancy dragon** — Save goods for enchantments first; grab fancy dragons as a finishing move.
